title: AI Follow-Up Suggestion (chip)
description: One deterministic follow-up chip inside the follow_up_suggestions component. Runtime source of truth: FollowUpSuggestion in runtime/app/agents/components.py — {id,label,message} is what ships today; `action` is the approved additive extension (ai-planning/19 §10, Phase B). Chips are built by module-router suggestion builders from validated tool output + conversation state — never by the LLM. `message` is the universal fallback: a frontend that ignores or fails `action` sends `message` as a plain user message. NOT to be confused with ai-suggestion.schema.json (the draft-for-approval write path).

label.description: Chip text. On an action click it is also echoed as the visible user message.

message.description: Plain-text fallback sent as a user message when `action` is absent or fails Laravel validation. Must be answerable by tools registered in the session's scope.

action.description: Optional structured action. Executes ONLY via POST /api/v1/tenant/ai/sessions/{id}/chat with {message: label, action} — Laravel re-validates before forwarding to the runtime as ui_action (type allow-listed; tool registered, in the session's module scope, and permitted for the user; every entity id in input ⊆ the session context_json's displayed_*/focused_entity_id). Invalid/stale actions get a 422 and the frontend falls back to `message`. Direct frontend→Python calls are impossible by design. Builders must never emit destructive actions — the action catalog is read-only by construction.

tool.description: A tool registered in AiToolRegistry and matching the session's module scope. Suggestion builders may only reference tools present in the request's tool_definitions (i.e. Laravel granted them).

input.description: Must validate against the tool's declared input_schema. Entity ids may reference only currently displayed/focused ids from conversation state — never guessed or historical ids.

ask_action.description: Explicit form of the fallback: send `message` as a plain user message.

journey.description: A journey name owned by a module router (currently pos_analytics, inventory, catalog — see runtime/app/agents/modules/). Used for empty-state journey starters.

follow_up_suggestions_props.description: Props of the follow_up_suggestions component: 1–4 chips, matching FollowUpSuggestionsProps in the runtime.
